homework.subject: physics
homework.question: A projectile is fired from the surface of the earth with a velocity of \( 5 \text{ m s}^{-1} \) and angle \( \theta \) with the horizontal. Another projectile fired from another planet with a velocity of \( 3 \text{ m s}^{-1} \) at the same angle follows a trajectory which is identical with the trajectory of the projectile fired from the earth. The value of the acceleration due to gravity on the planet is (in \( \text{m s}^{-2} \)) is (Given \( g = 9.8 \text{ m s}^{-2} \))
homework.hint: Equate the trajectory equations for both planets. For identical trajectories, \( \frac{g_1}{u_1^2} = \frac{g_2}{u_2^2} \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: The velocity of a projectile at the initial point A is \( (2\hat{i} + 3\hat{j})\text{m/s} \). Its velocity (in m/s) at point B is
homework.hint: In projectile motion, horizontal velocity remains constant, while vertical velocity reverses its direction at the same horizontal level.

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: The horizontal range and the maximum height of a projectile are equal. The angle of projection of the projectile is
homework.hint: Equate the formulas for horizontal range \( R = \frac{u^2 \sin 2\theta}{g} \) and maximum height \( H = \frac{u^2 \sin^2\theta}{2g} \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: A missile is fired for maximum range with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. If \( g = 10 \text{ m/s}^2 \), the range of the missile is
homework.hint: Maximum range occurs at a projection angle of \( 45^\circ \) and is calculated using \( R_{max} = \frac{u^2}{g} \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: A projectile is fired at an angle of \( 45^\circ \) with the horizontal. Elevation angle of the projectile at its highest point as seen from the point of projection, is
homework.hint: The angle of elevation \( \alpha \) is given by \( \tan \alpha = \frac{H}{R/2} \). Substitute the standard formulas for H and R at \( 45^\circ \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: The speed of a projectile at its maximum height is half of its initial speed. The angle of projection is
homework.hint: At maximum height, the speed is strictly the horizontal component \( u\cos\theta \). Equate this to \( \frac{u}{2} \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: A particle of mass m is projected with velocity v making an angle of \( 45^\circ \) with the horizontal. When the particle lands on the level ground the magnitude of the change in its momentum will be
homework.hint: Horizontal momentum remains constant. Change in vertical momentum is \( mv\sin\theta - (-mv\sin\theta) = 2mv\sin\theta \).

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: For angles of projection of a projectile at angle \( (45^\circ - \theta) \) and \( (45^\circ + \theta) \), the horizontal range described by the projectile are in the ratio of
homework.hint: The sum of the two angles is \( 90^\circ \). Complementary angles of projection yield the same horizontal range.

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: A particle A is dropped from a height and another particle B is projected in horizontal direction with speed of 5 m/s from the same height then correct statement is

options.text: particle A will reach at ground first with respect to particle B
particle B will reach at ground first with respect to particle A
both particles will reach at ground simultaneously
both particles will reach at ground with same speed.

homework.hint: The vertical motion determines time of flight and is independent of horizontal velocity. Both start with zero vertical velocity.

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: Two projectiles of same mass and with same velocity are thrown at an angle \( 60^\circ \) and \( 30^\circ \) with the horizontal, then which will remain same
homework.hint: The angles \( 60^\circ \) and \( 30^\circ \) sum to \( 90^\circ \). Projectiles with complementary angles have the same range.

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: If a body A of mass M is thrown with velocity v at an angle of \( 30^\circ \) to the horizontal and another body B of the same mass is thrown with the same speed at an angle of \( 60^\circ \) to the horizontal, the ratio of horizontal range of A to B will be
homework.hint: Since \( 30^\circ + 60^\circ = 90^\circ \), the bodies have complementary projection angles and therefore identical horizontal ranges.

homework.subject: physics
homework.question: The maximum range of a gun of horizontal terrain is 16 km. If \( g = 10 \text{ m s}^{-2} \), then muzzle velocity of a shell must be
homework.hint: Use the formula for maximum range \( R_{max} = \frac{v^2}{g} \) and solve for v.
